prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='CWT_catboost', seasonality_champion='ONLY_DWT_catboost', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: The validation folds show consistent downward trends across all periods, with CWT_catboost demonstrating superior trend tracking (lowest slope error) while ONLY_DWT_catboost leads in seasonality correlation. High model disagreement (0.7666) and substantial seasonality variance (0.5162) indicate that no single model dominates, supporting DBA combination as the optimal approach to leverage complementary strengths across early and late horizons.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "CWT_catboost",
  "seasonality_champion": "ONLY_DWT_catboost",
  "overall_champion": "ONLY_CWT_svr",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "ETS", "late": "ONLY_CWT_svr"},
  "tier1_models": ["ONLY_CWT_svr", "THETA", "ETS", "rf", "ONLY_FT_rf", "ARIMA", "ONLY_FT_catboost", "ONLY_FT_svr"],
  "tier2_models": ["CWT_catboost", "CWT_svr", "DWT_catboost", "DWT_rf", "CWT_rf", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "FT_svr"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.7666,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["down", "down", "down"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "The validation folds show consistent downward trends across all periods, with CWT_catboost demonstrating superior trend tracking (lowest slope error) while ONLY_DWT_catboost leads in seasonality correlation. High model disagreement (0.7666) and substantial seasonality variance (0.5162) indicate that no single model dominates, supporting DBA combination as the optimal approach to leverage complementary strengths across early and late horizons."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25", "best_per_horizon_by_validation"],
  "params_overrides": {"topk_mean_per_horizon_k3": {"top_k": 3}, "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25": {"top_k": 3, "shrinkage": 0.25}},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=8 indicates high model diversity across horizons, favoring selection methods like best_per_horizon_by_validation and topk_mean_per_horizon_k3. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for handling phase shifts (high_seasonality_variance=0.5162). With n_windows=3, smaller top_k=3 is appropriate to avoid overfitting. inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25 adds shrinkage for stability. RMSE spread ratio=0.7666 supports rmse_focus preset."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current set has 4 candidates with diverse types: 2 selection (topk_mean_per_horizon_k3, best_per_horizon_by_validation), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25), 1 baseline (dba_combination). Diversity rule satisfied (≥2 different types). No leakage detected - baselines never leak, selection uses validation data only, weighted candidate inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25 is in current set and appears to use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection per eval_config. Universe leaderboards show baseline_mean has best RMSE (12223.8) but current topk_mean_per_horizon_k3 already has score 0.90097 which is competitive. No redundant candidates (no ≥4 same-type). With n_windows=3, recommended knobs suggest stronger regularization - current weighted candidate with shrinkage=0.25 fits this. All names from valid_candidate_names.",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}
raw.statistician: {"add_names": [], "remove_names": [], "params_overrides": {}, "rationale": "Current candidate set is well-diversified with selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation), weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25), and baseline methods (dba_combination). Universe leaderboards show baseline_mean (RMSE: 12223.80) beats current best (topk_mean_per_horizon_k3 at RMSE: 12776.57), but adding it would reduce diversity. n_windows=3 is low, so recommended_knobs suggest stronger regularization - but we already have shrinkage=0.25 on inverse_rmse_weights. relative_spread_mean=0.12 < 0.25 threshold, so no need for DBA/trimmed mean. n_unique_winners=8 ≥ 3 justifies keeping best_per_horizon_by_validation. Stability is acceptable (RMSE_std/RMSE=0.14 < 0.3). No changes needed - current set balances performance and robustness appropriately.", "changes": [], "when_good": "When candidate set has diverse methods, n_windows ≤ 4, relative_spread_mean < 0.25, and stability acceptable (RMSE_std/RMSE < 0.3), no additions/removals needed."}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: The validation folds show consistent downward trends across all periods, with CWT_catboost demonstrating superior trend tracking (lowest slope error) while ONLY_DWT_catboost leads in seasonality correlation. High model disagreement (0.7666) and substantial seasonality variance (0.5162) indicate that no single model dominates, supporting DBA combination as the optimal approach to leverage complementary strengths across early and late horizons.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=8 indicates high model diversity across horizons, favoring selection methods like best_per_horizon_by_validation and topk_mean_per_horizon_k3. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for handling phase shifts (high_seasonality_variance=0.5162). With n_windows=3, smaller top_k=3 is appropriate to avoid overfitting. inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25 adds shrinkage for stability. RMSE spread ratio=0.7666 supports rmse_focus preset.

skeptic.rationale: Current set has 4 candidates with diverse types: 2 selection (topk_mean_per_horizon_k3, best_per_horizon_by_validation), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25), 1 baseline (dba_combination). Diversity rule satisfied (≥2 different types). No leakage detected - baselines never leak, selection uses validation data only, weighted candidate inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25 is in current set and appears to use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection per eval_config. Universe leaderboards show baseline_mean has best RMSE (12223.8) but current topk_mean_per_horizon_k3 already has score 0.90097 which is competitive. No redundant candidates (no ≥4 same-type). With n_windows=3, recommended knobs suggest stronger regularization - current weighted candidate with shrinkage=0.25 fits this. All names from valid_candidate_names.

statistician.rationale: Current candidate set is well-diversified with selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation), weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25), and baseline methods (dba_combination). Universe leaderboards show baseline_mean (RMSE: 12223.80) beats current best (topk_mean_per_horizon_k3 at RMSE: 12776.57), but adding it would reduce diversity. n_windows=3 is low, so recommended_knobs suggest stronger regularization - but we already have shrinkage=0.25 on inverse_rmse_weights. relative_spread_mean=0.12 < 0.25 threshold, so no need for DBA/trimmed mean. n_unique_winners=8 ≥ 3 justifies keeping best_per_horizon_by_validation. Stability is acceptable (RMSE_std/RMSE=0.14 < 0.3). No changes needed - current set balances performance and robustness appropriately.
statistician.when_good: When candidate set has diverse methods, n_windows ≤ 4, relative_spread_mean < 0.25, and stability acceptable (RMSE_std/RMSE < 0.3), no additions/removals needed.
